title: heuchera micrantha f. glaberrima
intro: Heuchera micrantha f. glaberrima, also known as Alabamum or American foxglove beardtongue, is a flowering plant native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Heuchera micrantha f. glaberrima include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and soothing digestive issues. Heuchera micrantha f. glaberrima can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for its medicinal uses, and it is also used in herbal infusions to promote overall health and well-being.
